mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates broad economic strength and robust corporate hiring, leading to higher aggregate demand, improved earnings expectations, and positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong job growth is a core component of economic expansion narratives.
failure_condition: The rank Information Coefficient of 3-month changes in PAYEMS with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate from its trailing 12-month low signals a negative inflection point in the labor market, often preceding a recession. This increases risk aversion and leads to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts a positive return over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation when the central bank is already raising the policy rate indicates the Fed is 'behind the curve'. This forces expectations of more aggressive future tightening, which sharply contracts equity valuation multiples.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY change > 3% and FEDFUNDS 3m change > 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:1970s stagflation period showed this dynamic.
failure_condition: The predictive power of the combined signal is not statistically superior to a model using only CPIAUCSL changes, as measured by out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production signals a robust rebound in corporate activity and profitability, driving positive short-term equity returns as earnings estimates are revised upwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in the first 24 months following an NBER-defined recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise in retail sales growth indicates stronger-than-expected consumer demand, leading to upward revisions in near-term earnings forecasts and a positive, short-lived reaction in equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approx. 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS surprises (defined as > 1 std dev above 12m avg) are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a high absolute threshold, it signifies restrictive monetary policy. This increases borrowing costs for corporations and raises the discount rate for future earnings, creating a headwind for equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:High policy rates preceded the 2000 and 2008 market downturns.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return when FEDFUNDS > 4% is not statistically lower than when FEDFUNDS <= 4% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year contraction in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and residential investment, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The housing sector is highly sensitive to interest rates and leads the business cycle.
failure_condition: Instances where HOUST YoY growth is below -15% are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days on average.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating real economic growth and high, persistent inflation creates a stagflationary environment. This compresses corporate profit margins and equity multiples, leading to negative returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1% AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 4%
failure_condition: The model's performance (e.g., hit rate) is not improved by adding the interaction term compared to separate models with only GDPC1 or CPIAUCSL.
